title: Micropiper alatum
taxonomy: Micropiper alatum is classified within the family Piperaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as pepper plants. This family is part of the order Piperales, which also includes other plant families such as Siparunaceae and Chloranthaceae. The genus Micropiper is a relatively small group of species, with only a few members identified to date. intro: Micropiper alatum, also known as Mountain Pepper, is a plant species native to New Caledonia, belonging to the Piperaceae family. The health benefits of Micropiper alatum include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include compounds such as piperonal, piperine, and lignans, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
